title: Use restrained semantic color variety
statement: Use product-native tokenized color with tasteful semantic variety; avoid both childish saturation and blanket desaturation or one-accent monotony.
rationale: Color should communicate meaningful status and structure while remaining professional and native to the product.

prefer: Use the product token system and reserve color differences for meaningful semantics.
Balance professional restraint with enough variety to distinguish states.
avoid: Making every state the same neutral or accent color.

when: Color carries state or status meaning in product UI.
not_when: A deliberately neutral or brandless wireframe is the artifact.

evidence.summary: Rejected saturated status colors as childish and asked for restrained, professional status treatment.

confidence.note: Bootstrapped from independent direct user episodes under the owner's request to mine durable design rules.

lifecycle.decision_note: Candidate pending independent recurrence or explicit owner approval.

positive_examples: Use the product token system and reserve color differences for meaningful semantics.
Balance professional restraint with enough variety to distinguish states.
negative_examples: Making every state the same neutral or accent color.

checks: Does each color difference carry meaning?
Does the palette feel product-native and professional?
